pattern: The two strings share every letter except the single inserted one, so any cancelling accumulator — frequency counts or a running XOR of code points — isolates the leftover character.
hints: Every letter of the shorter string appears in the longer one; only one extra letter is unmatched.
Counting occurrences in both strings and finding the letter whose counts differ pinpoints the addition.
XOR is even cleaner: XOR all character codes of both strings together and the matched pairs cancel, leaving the added letter's code.

edge_inputs.description: added letter duplicates an existing one
shuffled order with extra letter inserted in the middle

stress.description: shuffle a random base string then splice in one extra letter
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(0, size)
    base = [rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n)]
    s = ''.join(base)
    extra = rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase)
    base.append(extra)
    rng.shuffle(base)
    return [s, ''.join(base)]
stress.note: t is a shuffle of s plus exactly one added letter, matching the problem's generation
